title: Refute 'the test DB is broken' with a structured explanation
prompt: A developer reports: 'My test DB is corrupted. I keep dropping it and recreating it but tests still say records don't exist.' Write `docs/test_db_not_corrupted.md` structured as a decision tree: (1) is the test in a sandbox transaction? (yes, always) (2) does the test's own insert path succeed? (check the code) (3) does the test pass `{:ok, _}` from its create call? (verify the match) (4) is the assertion reading from a DIFFERENT process than the insert? (check for on_exit or spawned processes) — the real root cause is ALWAYS in one of (2)(3)(4), never in a corrupted DB. Must not suggest `mix ecto.drop`.
tier_rationale: Structured triage rejecting a plausible-but-wrong hypothesis. Requires enumeration of four alternative root causes.
